titleStmt.title: Letter from Patrick Higgins to sir Matthew Nathan, 10 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Patrick Higgins

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Patrick Higgins, assistant town clerk at Waterford Town Hall, to Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) , Under-Secretary for Ireland, on 10 January 1916. The letter is written on behalf of the mayor (presumably of Waterford) and is in regard to the mayor's recommendation to the Lord Lieutenant (Lord Wimborne) that Alderman William J Smith be appointed to the city magistracy.Sir Matthew Nathan as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Town Clerk's Office, Town Hall, Waterford 10th Jany., 1916. Patrick Higgins, Asst. Town Clerk. Sir With reference to your letter of the 27th
Nov., I am instructed by His Worship the Mayor to
ask you to be good enough to say when a reply may
be expected to his recommendation to His Excellency
the Lord Lieutenant of Alderman William J. Smith to
the City Magistracy. I am to add that many citizens consider that
Alderman Smith is the class of gentleman who would
make an intelligent and able Justice. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, Pat Higgins
Assistant Town Clerk. The Under-Secretary The Castle, DUBLIN.
